parsed_review.summary: CP00-668 (RP22 External Integrations I permission/audit/fixture bridge, Risk B, 40 units RP22.P01.M04.S07-RP22.P01.M06.S04) is a descriptor-only continuation pack with a sound evidence chain supporting eventual production_ready promotion. Verified via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) Scope/distribution match across review-baseline, registry requirements, and manifest plan_binding_snapshot - deliverables impl 22/ui 6/fixture 2/test 6/hermes 2/claude 2 and micro-titles Secondary Workflow Slice 14 / Permission And Audit Binding 22 / Synthetic Fixture Set 4 (sum 40). (2) Implementation is deterministic and consistent: createExternalIntegrationsICp668PermissionAuditFixtureBridgeDescriptor, validateExternalIntegrationsICp668PermissionAuditFixtureBridgeDescriptor, and validateExternalIntegrationsICp668Coverage are internally aligned; required public exports are reachable through index.js (export * from registry/service/validators); model.test.js asserts coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and on-disk contract equality. (3) Both contract files carry generated_from_pack_id CP00-668 with runtime boundaries closed on disk (permission_runtime_opened/audit_runtime_opened/external_api/hermes all false; claude_final_approval_claimed false). (4) Boundaries verified: tenant_id required on every entity, matter trace required, permission_decision_required_before_runtime + audit_hint_required true, cross_tenant_access_allowed false, serialization_hides_raw_external_payload true, blocked-claim routing for token_exposure/overbroad_sync/webhook_spoof; a secret/credential pattern grep across changed .js/.mjs/.json files returned no real tokens, keys, or payloads. (5) Authority boundary intact: Claude is read_only and not final approver, human approval required for runtime/enterprise-trust, Hermes packets are descriptor-only (hermes_runtime_opened false) and not embedded in product runtime. (6) No overclaim: manifest/construction-inspection/adjudication/command-evidence honestly report production_ready false and runtime_ready false pending this receipt, while command-evidence records the full validation matrix at exit_code 0. CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness auth_preflight passed, and the prior raw-output.json (status 143 SIGTERM, 0-byte stdout) was correctly NOT promoted to a valid receipt (exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run false, valid_review_receipts empty). No P0/P1 defects found. Three P3 informational observations are recorded below; none block pack or this-CP goal closeout.

findings.title: Descriptor validator falls back to a freshly generated case set when the passed case set is missing
findings.details: validateExternalIntegrationsICp668PermissionAuditFixtureBridgeDescriptor resolves caseSet via `descriptor.permission_audit_fixture_bridge_case_set ?? createExternalIntegrationsICp668PermissionAuditFixtureBridgeDescriptor().permission_audit_fixture_bridge_case_set`. If a caller passed a descriptor whose case set was stripped, the validator would validate a regenerated (always-valid) case set rather than the supplied one, so the section/row guards could pass on an incomplete descriptor. Risk is low: descriptors are deterministic factory outputs with no external input (input is void-ed), the same pattern is used by the already-closed CP666/CP667 validators, and the contract-projection validator performs a full JSON.stringify deep-equality against expectedProjection (exercised by the validator script and the on-disk contract test), which would catch a stripped case set.
findings.recommendation: Optional hardening: when a descriptor argument is provided, validate its own case_set (treat a missing case_set as an error) rather than substituting a freshly generated one. Track consistently across the CP666/CP667/CP668 validators if adopted.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; mitigated by deterministic factory construction and full projection equality check. No production_ready or closeout impact.

findings.title: CP00-668 coverage validates the manifest self-snapshot because the live plan packs[] no longer lists CP00-668
findings.details: The live docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json now references CP00-668 only under live_state (pack_number 668, primary_subphase RP22.P01.M06.S04); the forward-looking packs[] queue starts at CP00-669 / RP22.P01.M06.S05. Consequently `plan.packs.find(p => p.pack_id === 'CP00-668')` is undefined and the coverage check falls back to cp668Manifest.plan_binding_snapshot, making the coverage assertion self-referential against the pack's own snapshot rather than the live plan array. This matches the generator model (live pack moves to live_state once current) and the snapshot is bound to source_ledger_sha 13c7ed6..., so distribution integrity is still anchored to the ledger.
findings.recommendation: No change required. If stronger cross-checking is desired later, validate the live_state pointer plus the manifest snapshot against the ledger SHA explicitly.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; expected by design for the live/current pack. No closeout impact.

findings.title: Prior timed-out review run left a stale 0-byte raw-output not logged under invalid_review_attempts
findings.details: raw-output.json records an earlier review attempt with status 143 (SIGTERM, ~10 min) and stdout_bytes 0 — one of the CP178+ conditions that must not count as valid review evidence. The hardening behaved correctly: claude-review-result.json keeps status pending_pack_level_review with exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run false, valid_review_receipts empty, review_receipt_ref null, and readiness-report auth_preflight passed. However, invalid_review_attempts is empty rather than recording the timed-out 0-byte run, so the failed attempt is not explicitly enumerated. This artifact will be superseded by the current read-only run's output.
findings.recommendation: Consider recording timeout/SIGTERM 0-byte runs in invalid_review_attempts for a complete audit trail, so non-counted failed attempts are explicit rather than only implied by a null receipt.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; confirms hardening did not promote invalid evidence. No P0/P1 and no closeout impact; superseded by the current review receipt.
